title: Jakob Shurtleff’s CV
text:
Computational Physics Student, Programmer, Researcher.

jakobshurtleff@email.com | My GitHub Profile | Download PDF



6th Semester Computational Physics student focusing on semiconductor theory and AI integration.


Computational modeling, Python-based automation, multi-agent AI systems, and physics lab coordination.



Quantum mechanics, GAAFET/MOS capacitor simulation, astrophysics, and local data aggregation.




Sept 2022 - Apr 2026 Brigham Young University–Idaho, Rexburg, ID

Bachelor of Science in Physics
Computational Concentration




Programming Python (Polars, Scipy, Lets-Plot), C++, Ollama, Web Scraping.
Tools Linux, Git, Quarto, LaTeX, RTX-accelerated simulations.
Languages English (Native), Mandarin Chinese, Thai.





2026 Multi-Agent Chatbot Developed an interactive AI system using Python and Ollama for complex task delegation and reasoning.
2026 AI Fish Simulation Designed and debugged a reward-based AI system for aquatic behavioral simulations in C++.



2026 Local Event Scraper Built a Python-based web scraper to aggregate and format event data for Idaho Falls and Rexburg newsletters.




Feb 2026 - Present Physics Lab Coordinator, BYU-Idaho

Managed laboratory equipment and experimental setups.
Supported faculty in delivering undergraduate physics curriculum.

2025 - 2026 Independent Developer, Rexburg, ID

Optimized hardware-specific environments for RTX 2070 systems.
Developed and maintained custom Minecraft modpacks on NeoForge.
